question: In the Gospel of John, what does the narrator say was the reason the Jewish leaders sought to kill Jesus after he called God his Father?

choices.A: Because he was making himself equal with God
choices.B: Because he claimed to be a Roman citizen
choices.C: Because he said Moses was wrong
choices.D: Because he refused to do miracles

teaching.title: Narrator’s stated reason
teaching.body: John’s narrative states that calling God his Father was understood as “making himself equal with God.”
